case_id: Custom LCIA method for Transport process

pi_items.source: Task specification defines this as the custom method name to be created
Web search found no real LCIA method named 'Climate Priority'. This is a synthetic category created for the task.
Web search found that 'Acidification' is a real LCIA impact category, but 'Acidification Watch' is not a standard category name. This is a synthetic name created for the task.

pi_items.verified_value: Carbon dioxide (exists in USLCI, typically as 'Carbon dioxide, fossil')
pi_items.source: USLCI database documentation confirms carbon dioxide flows exist, documented as 'carbon dioxide, fossil' or 'carbon dioxide, biogenic' per submission handbook. USLCI is downloaded from GitHub (FLCAC-admin/uslci-content) in the environment setup script (install_openlca.sh, line 175).
USLCI database contains emission flows including sulfur dioxide as documented in LCIA documentation. Standard for acidification assessments per TRACI 2.1.
This is a synthetic normalization set name specified in the task description for demonstration purposes.
This is a synthetic weighting set name specified in the task description for demonstration purposes.
Output file path specified in task.json metadata and confirmed by setup script which creates /home/ga/LCA_Results directory.

Specified in task.json. This is a synthetic normalization factor for demonstration. Real normalization factors vary: global climate change normalization factor ~2.37E+13 kg CO2 eq per reference sources.
Specified in task.json. This is a synthetic normalization factor for demonstration purposes.

Specified in task.json. Plausible weighting factor value. Policy-based weighting methods vary, but allocating 80% weight to climate change and 20% to acidification is a reasonable example for demonstration.
Specified in task.json. Complementary to weight_climate (0.8 + 0.2 = 1.0), represents 20% weight for acidification impact category.

pi_items.metadata_value: 1.0 (implied from task description linking CO2 with factor 1.0)
pi_items.source: Standard LCIA characterization factor: CO2 has a characterization factor of 1 kg CO2-eq/kg CO2 by definition, as it is the reference substance for climate change impact category. Verified in LCIA documentation.

pi_items.metadata_value: 1.0 (implied from task description linking SO2 with factor 1.0)
pi_items.source: Standard LCIA characterization factor: SO2 has a characterization factor of 1 kg SO2-eq/kg SO2 by definition, as it is the reference substance for acidification potential. Verified in TRACI 2.1 documentation and LCIA methodology sources.

pi_items.verified_value: USLCI FY24 Q4 or FY25 Q1 from GitHub (FLCAC-admin/uslci-content)
pi_items.source: Environment installation script (install_openlca.sh, lines 173-187) downloads USLCI from: https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content/raw/dev/downloads/

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI v2.1 for FEDEFL flows from Figshare (file ID 46211520)
pi_items.source: Installation script downloads LCIA methods from Figshare ndownloader: https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/46211520 (TRACI v2.1, 8.4MB)

pi_items.metadata_value: 'Transport, combination truck, diesel powered' (mentioned in description)
pi_items.verified_value: 'Transport, combination truck, diesel powered' (expected in USLCI database)
pi_items.source: Task description specifies this process for product system creation. Referenced in OpenLCA case studies as a real process in USLCI/ecoinvent databases.

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on real USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) data but uses synthetic impact category names, normalization/weighting sets, and factors for demonstration. The method names 'Corporate Eco-Strategy 2025', 'Climate Priority', and 'Acidification Watch' are custom for this task—only 'Acidification' exists as a real LCIA category. Characterization factors of 1.0 for both CO2 and SO2 are scientifically correct (they are reference substances). The normalization factors (10000.0, 50.0) and weights (0.8, 0.2) are synthetic demonstration values. The task references the USLCI FY24 Q4 or FY25 Q1 database downloaded from GitHub and TRACI v2.1 LCIA methods from Figshare.
